cells.1: FORM BINDING DIRECTIVE — Diagnostic Wizard · Steps 1 / 2A / 2B / 2C / 2-TOD · v3.1 (May 2026)
Source of truth: cell-coordinate tables in sheets STEP-2A · STEP-2C · STEP-2B-L1 · STEP-2B-L2 · STEP-TOD · Build date: 2026-05-04 · Schema: ST_Canonical_Schema.xlsx v1.0
ABSOLUTE RULE

You are building forms for Steps 1, 2A, 2B, 2C, and 2-TOD of the Structural Typology diagnostic wizard. Every question text and every answer option text that appears in these forms MUST be read directly from the source XLSX files at the cell coordinates specified in the binding tables. Never paraphrase, shorten, or rewrite question text or answer options. The binding tables are the contract. If a binding table cell coordinate does not match the source file, STOP and report — do not improvise.
WHAT IS NEW IN v3.1

v3.1 implements the architectural rebuild of 2026-05-03. ALL prior binding coordinates are obsolete. v3.1 binding tables reflect: (1) New v3.1 sheet names: 3_Screening, 3_Level_1_Screening, 4_Level_2_Deepening; (2) New row positions — every screening question is followed by a Direct Observation Gate row before options; (3) New options — Option E ("cannot answer from direct observation") on every screening question, plus Option F on Q6/Q8/Q9/Q11 in AEM/TSAM and Q11 in EDv2 L1; (4) New questions — Q11 in AEM/TSAM, TED Q19 in Target Observed; (5) Schema dependency on ST_Canonical_Schema.xlsx for all enums; (6) Per-answer evidence machinery (EvidenceType, KnowledgeLevel, Confidence, ReliabilityFlags) handled by the application layer, NOT by the coder agent rendering the form options.
SOURCE FILES

Step 1 — Deal Context Setup: no questionnaire file. UI-only selectors (track, deal type, P1, P2). See sheet STEP-1.

Step 2A — Acquirer Environment Module:
 File: ST_Acquirer_Environment_Module.xlsx
 Sheet: 3_Screening (NEW v3.1 name — was "Screening" in v1)
 Questions: Q1–Q11 (11 total — Q11 is new in v3.1)

Step 2B — Target Environment Diagnostic (External Observer or Side-Agnostic):
 File: ST_Environment_Diagnostic_v2.xlsx
 Sheet 1: 3_Level_1_Screening (NEW v3.1 name — was "Level 1 — Screening" in v2)
 Questions: Q1–Q12 (12 total)
 Sheet 2: 4_Level_2_Deepening (NEW v3.1 name — was "Level 2 — Deepening" in v2)
 Questions: Q13–Q22 (10 total)

Step 2C — Target Self-Assessment Module:
 File: ST_Target_Self_Assessment_Module.xlsx
 Sheet: 3_Screening (NEW v3.1 name — was "Screening" in v1)
 Questions: Q1–Q11 (11 total — Q11 is new in v3.1)

Step 2-TOD — Target Observed Environment Diagnostic (acquirer-side observer instrument):
 File: ST_Target_Observed_Environment_Diagnostic.xlsx
 Sheet: Questionnaire (UNCHANGED — additive v3.1 rebuild)
 Questions: EVID Q1–Q4 + TED Q1–Q19 (23 total — TED Q19 is new in v3.1)
READING PROTOCOL — SCREENING SHEETS (AEM, TSAM, EDv2 L1, EDv2 L2)

For every TED-style question rendered in the form:

 1. Open the source file specified in the binding table.
 2. Navigate to the sheet specified in the binding table.
 3. Read the QUESTION TEXT from cell at (Question_row, column C). Note: text is in a merged region C:F; use the anchor cell C.
 4. Read each OPTION TEXT (A, B, C, D, E, optionally F) from cell at (Option_row, column C). Note: option text is in a merged region C:D; use the anchor cell C.
 5. The option LETTER itself is in column B for visual reference but is hard-coded in your binding — do not derive it from the file.

 6. v3.1 ADDITION — Direct Observation Gate:
 Before showing the options to the respondent, render the Direct Observation Gate:
 "Have you personally observed this in [your firm | your organisation | the target] in the last 24 months?"
 Three responses: yes / no / document_supported. The exact gate row coordinate is in the binding table (column "Gate_row").
 The gate prompt text is at (Gate_row, column 2) — anchor cell B of merged region B:C.

 7. v3.1 ADDITION — After option selection, capture per-answer evidence-machinery fields per ST_Canonical_Schema.xlsx Sheet 7_Answer_Record:
 evidenceType (enum)
 knowledgeLevel (enum)
 confidence (enum)
 reliabilityFlags (multi-select)
 observedRoles (free text, optional)
 evidenceItemIds (foreign keys, optional)
 Use the canonical-schema enum values exactly. Do NOT show the field labels visually as "Input" rows from the screening sheet — those rows are scaffolding for the practitioner-facing Excel view, not for the form.
READING PROTOCOL — STEP-TOD (Target Observed Environment Diagnostic)

STEP-TOD has a different cell convention from the screening sheets. ALL data for each question lives on ONE row:
 Col C = Question text
 Col D = Option A
 Col E = Option B
 Col F = Option C
 Col G = Option D
 Col Q = Option E (NEW v3.1 — column 17)

For every TED-style question in this sheet:
 1. Read question and 5 options from the row coordinates in STEP-TOD binding table.
 2. v3.1 — render Direct Observation Gate before options (use the same gate prompt as above).
 3. v3.1 — capture per-answer evidence machinery as above.

For EVID Q1–Q4 (evidence-calibration block at workbook level):
 Render before TED questions. EVID block does NOT need per-question Direct Observation Gate — the EVID block IS the evidence calibration. EVID answers feed the Evidence_Confidence sheet computation.
OPTION E SEMANTICS

Option E ("I cannot answer from direct observation") is a LEGITIMATE answer, not a failure mode. Render it normally as the fifth radio button. Selection of E:
 • Does not produce an environment signal (E_Environment = no_environment_signal in binding tables).
 • Does not block form progress — the respondent can submit forms with multiple E answers.
 • Triggers downstream "coverage_insufficient" engine flag if E count exceeds threshold per ST_Canonical_Schema sheet 14_RoleCoverageMatrix.
 • Per ST_Canonical_Schema sheet 7_Answer_Record validation: when respondent selects E, force evidenceType = unknown, knowledgeLevel = not_known, confidence = cannot_determine. Hide the evidence-machinery fields in this case (engine will set them to the unknown values automatically).

Option F (where present) follows the same semantics as E unless explicitly noted otherwise in the binding table column "Practitioner Note".
FORBIDDEN ACTIONS

You must NEVER:
 • Write question or answer text from memory or by paraphrasing.
 • Shorten, summarise, or improve wording.
 • Swap answer options between questions (e.g. use Q3-B text as a radio option for Q5).
 • Display a primary environment label as the form output. The form must not name an environment as the respondent's "type." Per ST_Canonical_Schema sheet 17_AnalystGate: paid outputs require analyst review; free-tier outputs may show a "directional pattern only" label and must NOT name a final environment.
 • Skip the Direct Observation Gate. The gate is a hard precondition for option selection in v3.1.
 • Persist evidence-machinery values that violate the canonical-schema validation rules in sheet 7_Answer_Record (e.g. evidenceType = direct_observation with confidence = cannot_determine is invalid).
 • Treat the practitioner-facing "Input" rows in the source file as form fields — those are Excel scaffolding for human reviewers, not API contracts.
 • Reuse cell coordinates from prior versions of the binding (v1.0, v1.1, v1.2). v3.1 invalidates them.
STRUCTURE OF BINDING TABLES

Each STEP binding sheet has these columns:
 Col A — Question number (Q1, Q2, …; or EVID Q1–Q4, TED Q1–Q19 in STEP-TOD)
 Col B — Option label: Q for question row · Gate for direct-observation gate row · A, B, C, D, E, F for answer rows
 Col C — Source file name
 Col D — Source sheet name
 Col E — Source row number
 Col F — Source column letter (C for screening sheets; D/E/F/G/Q for STEP-TOD options)
 Col G — Cell value snippet (first 80 chars) — VERIFY ONLY (read live from file at render time)
 Col H — Practitioner note (v3.1: rewrite flags, F-option presence, etc.)
STEP 1 — DEAL CONTEXT SETUP

Step 1 contains no questionnaire file content. It renders:
 • Track selector: "Do you represent the acquirer or the target?" (two buttons, no file source)
 • Deal type selector: Platform Acquisition / Operational Roll-Up / MBO / Cross-Border Integration (no file source)
 • P1 (acquirer track or target track): from Positioning sheet of AEM or TSAM
 • P2 (acquirer track or target track): from Positioning sheet of AEM or TSAM

See STEP-1 binding sheet for exact P1/P2 cell coordinates.

CRITICAL v3.1 CHANGE: Positioning sheets renamed from "Positioning" to "2_Positioning". Coordinates also changed because v3.1 has different layout. The v1.1 patch that fixed C→A for question label is preserved; v3.1 now ALSO fixes the option-text column (was misread as C in v1.1 — actually B with merged B:C).
ERROR HANDLING

If any of the following occur, STOP and report to the developer before rendering:
 • Source file cannot be opened.
 • Specified sheet does not exist in the file (especially v3.1 sheet rename: 3_Screening, 3_Level_1_Screening, 4_Level_2_Deepening).
 • Cell at the specified row/column is empty when content is expected.
 • Cell content does not start with the snippet in Col G of the binding table (drift detected).
 • Direct Observation Gate row missing from a screening question — indicates v3.1 architecture not deployed.
 • Option E missing from a screening question — same indication.
 • ST_Canonical_Schema.xlsx is not present at the expected path — schema dependency required.
SCHEMA DEPENDENCY (NEW IN v3.1)

All enum values rendered by the form (Side, EvidenceType, KnowledgeLevel, Confidence, ReliabilityFlag) MUST be sourced from ST_Canonical_Schema.xlsx as the single source of truth. Do not hard-code the enum lists. Do not paraphrase enum labels. Read them at application boot from the canonical schema sheets:
 • Sheet 1_Sides — Side enum
 • Sheet 2_Roles — Role taxonomy
 • Sheet 3_EvidenceType — EvidenceType enum
 • Sheet 4_KnowledgeLevel — KnowledgeLevel enum
 • Sheet 5_Confidence — Confidence enum
 • Sheet 6_ReliabilityFlags — Nine reliability flags

When the canonical schema updates, the form picks up the change automatically. Do not embed enum values in the binding tables, the application code, or the form templates.

cells.1: STEP-2B — Environment Diagnostic · Two-level routing · v3.1
FILE

ST_Environment_Diagnostic_v2.xlsx — single file, two screening sheets.
 Sheet 1: 3_Level_1_Screening (NEW v3.1 name — was "Level 1 — Screening" in v2) → Q1–Q12 (12 questions, 6 axes)
 Sheet 2: 4_Level_2_Deepening (NEW v3.1 name — was "Level 2 — Deepening" in v2) → Q13–Q22 (10 questions, 4 critical pairs)
LEVEL 1 — Q1 to Q12

Read from sheet: 3_Level_1_Screening (exact name — note underscore-separated v3.1 naming).
Binding table: sheet STEP-2B-L1 in this file.
Render all 12 questions in order Q1 → Q12. Each has 5 options (A–E) — Q11 has 6 (A–F).
Render Direct Observation Gate before each question's options.
LEVEL 2 — Q13 to Q22

Read from sheet: 4_Level_2_Deepening (exact name).
Binding table: sheet STEP-2B-L2 in this file.
Q13 starts at row 5 in this v3.1 layout (was row 6 in v2 source). Q numbering continues from Level 1 (Q13 not Q1).
Render all 10 questions in order Q13 → Q22. Each has 5 options (A–E).
Render Direct Observation Gate before each question's options.
CRITICAL — ROW NUMBERS ARE NOT SEQUENTIAL

The source files contain header rows, axis-label rows, gate rows, input-field rows, methodology-note rows, and spacer rows between questions. Row numbers are NOT sequential with question numbers. v3.1 layout has approximately 19 rows between consecutive questions (was 5–7 rows in v2). Use the binding tables for exact coordinates — never guess.

Example v3.1 layout pattern per question:
 Row N: Axis bar
 Row N+1: Question stem (anchor cell column C of merged C:F)
 Row N+2: Direct Observation Gate row
 Rows N+3 to N+7: Options A–E (anchor cells column C of merged C:D)
 Rows N+8 to N+15: Practitioner Input fields (NOT to be rendered as form fields)
 Rows N+16, N+17: Methodology notes
 Row N+18: Spacer
COVERAGE WARNING (v3.1 ESCALATION)

When the engine produces a primary or secondary signal for one of the THIN-COVERAGE environments, route the form output through dedicated environment questionnaires for confirmation:

 NT/STP (8/22 signals) → NT_STP_Diagnostic_Questionnaire.xlsx
 NF/NT (8/22 signals) → NF_NT_Diagnostic_Questionnaire.xlsx
 NF/SFP (3/22 signals) → NF_SFP_Diagnostic_Questionnaire.xlsx

Per ST_Environment_Diagnostic_v2.xlsx sheet 5_Reliability_Audit and sheet 6_Layered_Scoring: the instrument flags these environments as instrument artifacts when they emerge from this 22-question screen. Do NOT issue a primary-environment output for thin-coverage environments without dedicated-questionnaire confirmation.
